summary: The data indicates an approximately normal distribution of IOP values among glaucoma patients, meaning most measurements fall within two standard deviations of the mean. Given this information, it is reasonable to expect that 95% of cases will have an IOP between 28 and 32 mm Hg, making the lower bound close to 28 mm Hg when considering only one tail of the curve at the specified confidence level. To provide more precision would require specific statistical calculations or tables not provided here but generally accepted methods suggest using z-scores related to percentiles under such conditions.
